Title: Progress in the dissection and manipulation of vitamin E synthesis

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Nicotiana tabacum; Arabidopsis thaliana; Glycine max
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: TyrA expression alone had minor effects on tocochromanol levels, but when co-overexpressed with HPPD strongly increased total tocochromanols, largely as tocotrienols.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transgenic overexpression; co-overexpression with HPPD; metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TyrA bypasses feedback inhibition of arogenate dehydrogenase by producing HPP directly from prephenate. 2. TyrA alone causes only minor changes in tocochromanols. 3. TyrA plus HPPD markedly increases tocochromanols in tobacco leaves and Arabidopsis/soybean seed, mostly as tocotrienols.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful with HPPD to boost vitamin E pathway flux, but not sufficient alone.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Arabidopsis and soybean seeds co-overexpressing TyrA and HPPD accumulated extremely high HGA, became black from oxidative polymerization, and showed impaired germination.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This is a review paper summarizing transgenic studies; TyrA is not a native plant core pathway gene but an engineering gene used to increase precursor supply.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: whole genome sequencing; global gene expression analysis; metabolite profiling (no accession provided)

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Nicotiana tabacum; Glycine max
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts HPP to homogentisic acid, supplying the aromatic headgroup for tocopherol and tocotrienol synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: required but not a major single limiting step alone in Arabidopsis and tobacco; flux limitation becomes apparent when precursor supply is also increased with TyrA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of PDS1 abolishes both tocochromanol and plastoquinone biosynthesis in Arabidopsis; overexpression causes only modest increases in tocopherols alone but much larger increases when combined with TyrA.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant analysis; ortholog comparison; overexpression; enzyme activity assay; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Arabidopsis PDS1/HPPD is essential for tocochromanol and plastoquinone biosynthesis. 2. Overexpression of HPPD alone gives only modest increases in tocopherols. 3. Co-overexpression with TyrA strongly increases total tocochromanols, indicating precursor flux to HGA can limit pathway output when HPP supply is enhanced.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: With TyrA co-overexpression, very high HGA accumulation caused black seeds and impaired germination in Arabidopsis and soybean.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The single Synechocystis ortholog affects tocopherol but not plastoquinone synthesis, implying an HGA-independent plastoquinone pathway in Synechocystis.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: whole genome sequencing; metabolite profiling (no accession provided)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for increasing vitamin E when stacked with TyrA or other flux-enhancing genes; HPPD alone has limited value for large tocopherol gains.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: antioxidant; essential nutrient source for diet in photosynthetic organisms
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts HPP to homogentisic acid for tocopherol synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: essential step; rate limitation not established
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mutation affects tocopherol synthesis but not plastoquinone synthesis in Synechocystis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. slr0090 is the Synechocystis HPPD ortholog. 2. Mutation disrupts tocopherol synthesis. 3. Unlike Arabidopsis, plastoquinone synthesis is not affected, suggesting an alternative pathway there.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Located in a putative ten-gene operon used to identify neighboring tocopherol genes.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Primarily useful as a comparative model gene for pathway dissection.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: essential human dietary nutrient; antioxidant; protects storage lipids during germination
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Prenylates homogentisic acid with phytyl-diphosphate to commit flux to tocopherol synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: committed-step enzyme directly required for tocopherol branch
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: first committed and limiting activity for tocopherol synthesis in leaves and seeds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Null mutation causes complete tocopherol deficiency in leaves and seeds; overexpression increases total tocopherols up to fivefold in leaves and twofold in seeds.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ortholog isolation; null mutant analysis; overexpression; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VTE2 encodes the sole Arabidopsis HPT responsible for tocopherol synthesis. 2. Null mutants are completely tocopherol deficient in leaves and seeds. 3. Overexpression increases tocopherol levels, showing VTE2 is limiting. 4. Combined overexpression with VTE4 additively boosts total tocopherols and vitamin E activity.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: vte2 mutants show massive oxidation of storage lipids during germination and severely reduced fitness.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: HPT activity was also limiting for leaf tocopherol synthesis under combined high light and nutrient stress.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: combined high light and nutrient stress
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: whole genome sequencing; metabolite profiling (no accession provided)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target to increase total tocopherol content; especially valuable combined with VTE4 to raise both amount and vitamin E quality.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Prenylates homogentisate using PDP and can also use GGDP.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: committed-step enzyme for prenylated tocochromanol intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: essential step; rate limitation not established
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Disruption causes deficiency of all tocopherols and prenylated pathway intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: genome query; homologous recombination disruption; metabolite analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. slr1736 was identified as a ChlG-related polyprenyltransferase. 2. Gene disruption eliminates tocopherols and prenylated intermediates. 3. The enzyme can use both PDP and GGDP despite Synechocystis not accumulating tocotrienols.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Found in a two-gene operon with tocopherol cyclase gene slr1737.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: whole genome sequencing; metabolite profiling (no accession provided)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful comparative enzyme for understanding substrate specificity of prenylation.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Nicotiana tabacum; Zea mays
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: geranylgeranylated homogentisate intermediates leading to tocotrienols
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Prenylates homogentisate with GGDP to direct flux into tocotrienol synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: branch-switch enzyme directing flux toward tocotrienols
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key determinant of tocotrienol production in tissues
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression causes large increases in tocotrienols in Arabidopsis leaves, tobacco cell cultures, and maize embryos.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous overexpression in plants/cell cultures; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Monocot HPT paralogs encode HGGT activity. 2. Overexpression specifically causes large increases in tocotrienols, especially γ-tocotrienol. 3. Barley HGGT in Arabidopsis leaves produced tocotrienols at 10–15-fold above total tocochromanol levels of controls. 4. In maize embryos, HGGT overexpression raised tocotrienols to 4–6 times wild-type tocopherol levels.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Vitamin E activity increased only marginally in Arabidopsis leaves because the accumulated product was mainly γ-tocotrienol with lower vitamin E activity.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Active proteins could not be produced in E. coli for direct enzymatic studies; activity was inferred from transgenic product profiles.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong tool to engineer tocotrienol-rich crops, especially monocot seeds and heterologous tissues.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: DMPBQ and methylated intermediates leading to α/γ forms
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Methylates 2-methyl-6-phytylplastoquinol pathway intermediates to influence downstream tocopherol composition.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product composition
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: not a major regulator of total pathway flux but essential for determining tocochromanol types
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Seed-specific overexpression with Arabidopsis VTE4 in soybean enabled near-complete conversion of β, γ and δ tocopherols to α-tocopherol and markedly increased vitamin E activity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: map-based cloning of partial loss-of-function alleles; enzymatic inference; transgenic overexpression with VTE4; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VTE3 was the last tocopherol pathway enzyme cloned in Arabidopsis. 2. It does not strongly control total flux but is important for determining tocochromanol composition. 3. Co-expression with VTE4 in soybean strongly shifted tocopherol composition to α-tocopherol and increased oil vitamin E activity.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Arabidopsis VTE3 shares <20% amino acid identity with Synechocystis SLL0418 despite similar activity, indicating convergent evolution.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: seed-specific promoter in soybean overexpression study
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: map-based cloning; metabolite profiling (no accession provided)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable for engineering α-tocopherol-rich oils when stacked with VTE4.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Methylates MPBQ-type intermediates in tocopherol biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Identified as the Synechocystis MPBQ MT based on sequence similarity to γ-TMT and E. coli enzymatic analysis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: sequence similarity; E. coli expression and enzyme analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. sll0418 encodes Synechocystis MPBQ MT. 2. It was identified by similarity to γ-TMT. 3. Enzymatic analysis of recombinant protein confirmed activity.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The cyanobacterial sequence was not useful for identifying the plant ortholog because of strong divergence.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Mainly important as a comparative model enzyme.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes prenylquinol intermediates to form the chromanol ring of tocopherols.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: not established generally as rate-limiting, but overexpression strongly increases leaf tocochromanols
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mutation abolishes tocopherol synthesis and causes DMPBQ accumulation; overexpression in leaves increases tocochromanols sevenfold, mostly as γ-tocopherol.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant identification; chromosome walking; recombinant enzyme assay; overexpression; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VTE1 encodes tocopherol cyclase. 2. Loss of VTE1 eliminates tocopherols and accumulates DMPBQ. 3. Overexpression unexpectedly raises γ-tocopherol rather than α-tocopherol in Arabidopsis leaves. 4. DMPBQ accumulated in vte1 can compensate for tocopherol absence in seed lipid protection during germination.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: VTE1 overexpressing leaves had reduced ascorbate and glutathione levels; broader physiological consequences remain unclear.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Arabidopsis vte1 mutants under standard conditions showed little visible phenotype, unlike maize sxd1 and potato RNAi cyclase suppression lines.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: map-based/chromosome walking; metabolite profiling (no accession provided)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can strongly elevate total tocopherols, but product profile and possible antioxidant-network perturbation should be considered.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes prenylquinol intermediates in tocopherol synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: essential step; rate limitation not established
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Identified as the Synechocystis ortholog of Arabidopsis VTE1.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ortholog identification based on operon context and sequence relationship
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. slr1737 is the Synechocystis tocopherol cyclase ortholog. 2. It is colocated with slr1736 in a two-ORF operon. 3. Comparative genomics facilitated its identification.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review does not provide detailed mutant phenotype here.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Comparative pathway gene for model-system dissection.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: highest vitamin E activity among tocopherols; antioxidant
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Methylates γ-tocopherol to α-tocopherol, determining final vitamin E composition and activity.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: not a major controller of total flux but decisive for final α-tocopherol content and vitamin E activity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Seed-specific overexpression in Arabidopsis converted near all γ-tocopherol to α-tocopherol, causing an ~80-fold increase in α-tocopherol and a ninefold increase in vitamin E activity; additive with VTE2 for total vitamin E improvement.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene disruption of ortholog; E. coli expression; seed-specific overexpression; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VTE4 encodes γ-TMT. 2. It is essential for converting γ-tocopherol to α-tocopherol. 3. Seed-specific overexpression in Arabidopsis nearly completely converts γ- to α-tocopherol. 4. Co-expression with VTE2 additively increases both total tocopherols and vitamin E activity. 5. Co-expression with VTE3 in soybean strongly shifts tocopherol composition to α-tocopherol.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In Arabidopsis leaves, γ-TMT was generally not limiting except during severe abiotic stress according to prior work summarized by the review.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: severe abiotic stress (context for leaf limitation)
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Arabidopsis seed predominantly accumulates γ-tocopherol in wild type, implying limited endogenous terminal methylation there
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: seed-specific overexpression studied; leaves in combination studies
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: seed-specific promoter in Arabidopsis and soybean engineering studies
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Premier engineering target to improve vitamin E quality by enriching α-tocopherol; especially powerful with VTE2 or VTE3.

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: not established as total flux control
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Disruption causes loss of α-tocopherol and accumulation of γ-tocopherol.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: homologous recombination disruption; E. coli expression of enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. slr0089 was identified adjacent to slr0090 in a putative operon. 2. Disruption eliminated α-tocopherol and caused γ-tocopherol accumulation. 3. Recombinant protein assays confirmed γ-TMT activity.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Its operon context with HPPD helped identify tocopherol biosynthetic genes in Synechocystis.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: genome analysis; enzyme assay (no accession provided)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Comparative model gene for vitamin E composition control.
